title: Not framing the agent as a pro
one_liner: Treating the agent like a generic chatbot — no role, no standards — invites confident hallucination.
problem: Without a role frame ("You are a senior X working on Y"), the model falls back on its statistical centre of gravity: average code, average prose, average judgement. It also loses the licence to say "I don't know" — because a generic helper is supposed to help. Result: confidently wrong output, plausibly formatted, hard to spot in review.
solution: Frame the agent at the top of the system prompt: who they are, what bar they hold themselves to, what they should refuse, when to ask before guessing. "Senior backend engineer; production safety > speed; if unsure, list options instead of choosing." Pair with explicit permission to say "I don't have enough context".
